title: MITRE ATLAS — Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems
description: MITRE ATLAS is a globally accessible, living knowledge base of adversary tactics and techniques against AI/ML systems. Analogous to MITRE ATT&CK but specifically for AI attack surfaces.

tactics.name: Reconnaissance
tactics.description: Adversary gathers information about the AI system to plan future operations. Includes identifying AI capabilities, research efforts, model types used.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Attackers may probe legal AI tools to identify underlying models, training data, or system prompt content before launching more targeted attacks.

tactics.name: Resource Development
tactics.description: Adversary creates, purchases, or compromises AI artifacts, infrastructure, accounts, or capabilities to support operations.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Competitors or adversaries may develop adversarial documents or prompts specifically targeting a legal AI tool's known weaknesses.

tactics.name: Initial Access
tactics.description: Adversary gains initial access to the AI system via various entry vectors.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Unauthorized access to a legal AI platform's API or admin interface.

tactics.name: AI Model Access
tactics.description: Adversary gains varying levels of access to an AI model — from full internals knowledge to API access to indirect interaction through a product.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Legal AI tools that expose model APIs without proper authentication enable model extraction or adversarial probing.

tactics.name: Execution
tactics.description: Adversary runs malicious code embedded in AI artifacts or software.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Malicious code in AI-generated output (e.g., code suggestions) or compromised model artifacts.

tactics.name: Persistence
tactics.description: Adversary maintains foothold via AI artifacts or software — e.g., poisoned training data or manipulated models.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Backdoored legal AI models that persist malicious behavior across deployments.

tactics.name: Privilege Escalation
tactics.description: Adversary gains higher-level permissions by exploiting system weaknesses or misconfigurations.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Escalating from client-level to attorney-level access in a legal platform.

tactics.name: Defense Evasion
tactics.description: Adversary avoids detection by AI-enabled security software.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Crafting inputs that bypass content filters in legal AI tools while still achieving malicious objectives.

tactics.name: Credential Access
tactics.description: Adversary steals account names, passwords, or API keys — including through system prompt extraction.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Extracting API keys or credentials embedded in system prompts of legal AI tools.

tactics.name: Discovery
tactics.description: Adversary tries to figure out the AI environment — model architecture, training data, system configuration.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Probing legal AI tools to discover what client data they have access to, what databases they connect to, or what the system prompt instructs.

tactics.name: Collection
tactics.description: Adversary collects data of interest including training data, model outputs, and sensitive application data.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Exfiltrating client matter data, privileged communications, or attorney work product via AI system vulnerabilities.

tactics.name: Exfiltration
tactics.description: Adversary steals data or model artifacts from the AI system.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Model theft (replicating proprietary legal AI) or client data exfiltration via compromised AI pipeline.

tactics.name: Impact
tactics.description: Adversary manipulates, interrupts, or destroys systems and data, including corrupting model outputs or denying service.
tactics.legal_tech_relevance: Corrupting legal AI outputs to cause incorrect legal analysis, denying service to critical deadline-driven legal workflows, or manipulating AI-assisted filings.

high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.name: LLM Prompt Injection
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.description: Attacker crafts inputs that override or manipulate LLM system instructions.
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.legal_relevance: Direct attack vector against legal AI tools processing adversarial documents

high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.name: LLM Jailbreak
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.description: Bypassing safety restrictions or ethical guidelines of an LLM.
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.legal_relevance: Bypassing content filters in legal advice tools to extract improper guidance

high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.name: Valid Accounts
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.description: Using legitimate credentials to gain access.
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.legal_relevance: Compromised attorney credentials used to access privileged client matter data

high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.name: Craft Adversarial Data
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.description: Creating data specifically designed to manipulate AI model behavior.
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.legal_relevance: Crafting adversarial contracts designed to manipulate AI contract review tools

high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.name: Exfiltration via ML Inference API
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.description: Using model inference to extract training data or client data.
high_priority_techniques_for_legal_ai.legal_relevance: Extracting client data memorized by fine-tuned legal AI models

note: Full ATLAS data including all 84 techniques and 42 case studies is available in ATLAS.yaml in this directory. The YAML is the authoritative source.
